tasks.title: Create crates/util/ directory structure
tasks.description: Create the directory structure for the opencode-util crate including src/ and tests/ directories with all required module files (logging.rs, error.rs, retry.rs, fs.rs, helpers.rs) and tests/util_tests.rs
test_criteria: AddRegressionTest: verify directory structure exists after creation
AddUnitTest: verify all required files are created
test_commands: test -d crates/util/src && test -f crates/util/src/lib.rs && test -f crates/util/src/logging.rs && test -f crates/util/src/error.rs && test -f crates/util/src/retry.rs && test -f crates/util/src/fs.rs && test -f crates/util/src/helpers.rs && echo 'Directory structure valid'
tasks.impl_notes: Create all directories and placeholder files as specified in the module structure

tasks.title: Update workspace Cargo.toml
tasks.description: Add opencode-util crate to workspace members, add opencode-util to workspace.dependencies, add tracing-appender dependency, and update tracing-subscriber with json and env-filter features
test_criteria: AddRegressionTest: verify workspace compiles with new crate
AddUnitTest: verify Cargo.toml has correct entries
tasks.impl_notes: Must add 'crates/util' to members array, opencode-util to workspace.dependencies, tracing-appender = '0.2', and update tracing-subscriber features

tasks.title: Create crates/util/Cargo.toml
tasks.description: Create Cargo.toml for opencode-util with all required dependencies: tokio, tracing, tracing-appender, tracing-subscriber, thiserror, serde, serde_json, rand, anyhow, walkdir, and dev-dependencies tempfile and tokio-test
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify Cargo.toml parses correctly
AddRegressionTest: verify crate builds with dependencies
tasks.impl_notes: Use exact versions as specified in the task specification

tasks.title: Create crates/util/src/lib.rs with re-exports
tasks.description: Create lib.rs with module declarations and re-exports for Logger, LogLevel, Rotation, log_file_path, NamedError, WithContext, Context, RetryConfig, retry, atomic_write, read_json, write_json, ensure_dir, Lazy, with_timeout, wait_for, retry_until, and iife! macro
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify lib.rs compiles
AddRegressionTest: verify all re-exports are accessible
tasks.impl_notes: Follow the exact re-export structure specified

tasks.title: Implement LogLevel enum (FR-422)
tasks.description: Implement LogLevel enum with Debug, Info, Warn, Error variants. Must derive Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize. Must use #[serde(rename_all = 'lowercase')]
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify LogLevel variants exist
AddUnitTest: verify LogLevel Serializes to lowercase strings
AddUnitTest: verify LogLevel Deserializes from lowercase strings
AddUnitTest: verify LogLevel derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq
tasks.impl_notes: Use serde derive macros and rename_all = 'lowercase' attribute

tasks.title: Implement Logger struct with fluent API (FR-423-428)
tasks.description: Implement Logger struct with fields: level (LogLevel), file_path (Option<PathBuf>), console (bool). Must implement fluent API: with_level(), with_file(), with_no_console(), and init() method. init() must return Result<(), SetLoggerError>
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify Logger::with_level() creates Logger with specified level
AddUnitTest: verify Logger::with_file() sets file_path
AddUnitTest: verify Logger::with_no_console() disables console output
AddUnitTest: verify Logger::init() initializes tracing subscriber
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify init() returns SetLoggerError on multiple init attempts
tasks.impl_notes: Use tracing-subscriber with appropriate fmt layer. Ensure file rotation works if P2-1 is implemented

tasks.title: Implement NamedError struct (FR-431)
tasks.description: Implement NamedError struct with fields: name (String), code (Option<String>), message (String), data (Option<serde_json::Value>). Must derive Debug, Clone, Error. Must implement new(), with_code(), with_data(), kind() methods. Must implement Display trait
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify NamedError::new() creates error with name and message
AddUnitTest: verify NamedError::with_code() adds code field
AddUnitTest: verify NamedError::with_data() adds data field
AddUnitTest: verify NamedError::kind() returns name
AddUnitTest: verify NamedError Display shows 'name: message' format
AddUnitTest: verify NamedError implements Error trait
tasks.impl_notes: Use thiserror for Error derive. Ensure Serialize/Deserialize derives work correctly

tasks.title: Implement From<std::io::Error> for NamedError (FR-432)
tasks.description: Implement From trait to convert std::io::Error into NamedError with name='io' and message from error description
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify io::Error converts to NamedError with name='io'
AddUnitTest: verify error message is preserved in conversion
AddUnitTest: verify error chain is preserved
tasks.impl_notes: Use io::Error to_string() for message

tasks.title: Implement From<reqwest::Error> for NamedError (FR-433)
tasks.description: Implement From trait to convert reqwest::Error into NamedError with name='reqwest' and message from error description
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify reqwest::Error converts to NamedError with name='reqwest'
AddUnitTest: verify error message is preserved in conversion
AddUnitTest: verify url is accessible if present
tasks.impl_notes: Use reqwest error to_string() for message

tasks.title: Implement WithContext<E> wrapper (FR-434)
tasks.description: Implement WithContext<E> wrapper struct that wraps an inner error E and a NamedError context. Must implement Deref to access inner error, Display that shows context message, and Error trait
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify WithContext wraps inner error
AddUnitTest: verify WithContext derefs to inner error
AddUnitTest: verify WithContext Display shows context message
AddUnitTest: verify WithContext implements Error trait
tasks.impl_notes: Use std::error::Error and Deref trait

tasks.title: Implement Context trait (FR-435)
tasks.description: Implement Context trait with context() method that takes any error and wraps it with a NamedError. Should be implemented for std::io::Error and other error types
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify context() method on io::Error returns WithContext
AddUnitTest: verify context() method adds NamedError context
AddUnitTest: verify Context trait works with multiple error types
tasks.impl_notes: Implement as extension trait for std::error::Error

tasks.title: Implement RetryConfig struct (FR-437)
tasks.description: Implement RetryConfig struct with fields: max_attempts (u32), base_delay (Duration), max_delay (Duration), jitter (bool). Must implement Default with sensible defaults (3 attempts, 100ms base, 30s max, jitter enabled)
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify RetryConfig::default() creates config with sensible defaults
AddUnitTest: verify RetryConfig fields are accessible
AddUnitTest: verify custom RetryConfig can be created
tasks.impl_notes: Use Duration from std::time

tasks.title: Implement retry() function (FR-438)
tasks.description: Implement retry() async function that takes an async operation and RetryConfig, executes operation up to max_attempts times with exponential backoff and optional jitter, returns Result of inner type or last error
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify retry succeeds on first attempt when operation succeeds
AddUnitTest: verify retry retries on failure and eventually succeeds
AddUnitTest: verify retry returns error after max_attempts
AddUnitTest: verify retry applies exponential backoff
AddUnitTest: verify retry applies jitter when configured
tasks.impl_notes: Use rand for jitter. Implement proper backoff calculation

tasks.title: Implement read_to_string() (FR-441)
tasks.description: Implement async read_to_string() function that reads file contents to String using tokio::fs
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify read_to_string reads file contents
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify read_to_string returns error for non-existent file
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify read_to_string handles permission errors
tasks.impl_notes: Use tokio::fs::read_to_string

tasks.title: Implement write() (FR-442)
tasks.description: Implement async write() function that writes String contents to file using tokio::fs
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify write creates new file with content
AddUnitTest: verify write overwrites existing file
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify write handles directory not found errors
tasks.impl_notes: Use tokio::fs::write

tasks.title: Implement atomic_write() (FR-443)
tasks.description: Implement atomic_write() that writes content to temp file first, then renames to target path for atomicity. Must use tempfile or similar for staging
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify atomic_write creates file atomically
AddRegressionTest: verify atomic_write does not leave partial files on failure
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify atomic_write handles rename errors
tasks.impl_notes: Write to temp file in same directory, then rename for atomicity

tasks.title: Implement ensure_dir() (FR-444)
tasks.description: Implement ensure_dir() async function that creates directory and all parent directories if they don't exist
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify ensure_dir creates directory
AddUnitTest: verify ensure_dir creates parent directories recursively
AddUnitTest: verify ensure_dir succeeds if directory already exists
tasks.impl_notes: Use tokio::fs::create_dir_all

tasks.title: Implement read_json() (FR-445)
tasks.description: Implement read_json<T: Deserialize>() async function that reads file and deserializes JSON content to specified type using NamedError for error handling
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify read_json deserializes valid JSON
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify read_json returns NamedError for invalid JSON
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify read_json returns NamedError for missing file
tasks.impl_notes: Use serde_json::from_reader with error conversion

tasks.title: Implement write_json() (FR-446)
tasks.description: Implement write_json<T: Serialize>() async function that serializes value to JSON and writes to file with pretty formatting using NamedError for error handling
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify write_json serializes and writes valid JSON
AddUnitTest: verify write_json output is pretty formatted
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify write_json returns NamedError on serialization error
tasks.impl_notes: Use serde_json::to_string_pretty and atomic_write

tasks.title: Implement Rotation struct (FR-423)
tasks.description: Implement Rotation struct for log file rotation configuration. Should support options like None, Daily, Hourly, or size-based rotation
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify Rotation::none() disables rotation
AddUnitTest: verify Rotation::daily() creates daily rotation config
AddUnitTest: verify Rotation fields are accessible
tasks.impl_notes: Should integrate with tracing-appender for actual rotation

tasks.title: Implement log_file_path() (FR-429)
tasks.description: Implement log_file_path() function that returns PathBuf for log file location, accepting rotation configuration and computing appropriate filename with date/time suffixes
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify log_file_path returns valid PathBuf
AddUnitTest: verify log_file_path includes rotation timestamp when configured
AddUnitTest: verify log_file_path handles different rotation modes
tasks.impl_notes: Use chrono for timestamp formatting

tasks.title: Add tracing-appender dependency
tasks.description: Add tracing-appender = '0.2' to workspace dependencies in Cargo.toml
test_criteria: AddRegressionTest: verify tracing-appender dependency is present
AddRegressionTest: verify workspace compiles
tasks.impl_notes: Already specified in P0-2, ensure it's present

tasks.title: Implement log file rotation with Rotation (FR-423)

tasks.title: Implement Lazy<T> struct (FR-448)
tasks.description: Implement Lazy<T> struct for lazily initialized values. Must be thread-safe (Sync + Send), initialize only once using Once, and provide get_or_init() method
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify Lazy<T> is Send + Sync
AddUnitTest: verify Lazy<T> initializes only once
AddUnitTest: verify Lazy<T> get_or_init() returns reference to value
AddUnitTest: verify Lazy<T> can be used in static context
tasks.impl_notes: Use std::sync::Once or once_cell::sync::Lazy

tasks.title: Implement iife! macro (FR-449)
tasks.description: Implement iife! macro for immediately invoked function expressions. Macro should accept a closure and execute it immediately, returning the result
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify iife! macro executes closure immediately
AddUnitTest: verify iife! macro returns closure result
AddUnitTest: verify iife! macro works with different return types
tasks.impl_notes: Use macro_rules! for declarative macro

tasks.title: Implement with_timeout() (FR-450)
tasks.description: Implement with_timeout() async function that executes future with a timeout, returning Result<T, Elapsed> on timeout using tokio::time::timeout
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify with_timeout() returns value when future completes within timeout
AddUnitTest: verify with_timeout() returns Elapsed when future exceeds timeout
AddUnitTest: verify with_timeout() works with different Duration values
tasks.impl_notes: Use tokio::time::timeout

tasks.title: Implement wait_for() (FR-451)
tasks.description: Implement wait_for() async function that waits for a condition to become true, checking periodically with specified interval, returning when condition is met or timeout is exceeded
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify wait_for() returns when condition becomes true
AddUnitTest: verify wait_for() returns error when timeout exceeded
AddUnitTest: verify wait_for() checks condition at correct intervals
tasks.impl_notes: Use interval checking with tokio::time::interval

tasks.title: Implement retry_until() (FR-452)
tasks.description: Implement retry_until() async function that retries an operation until a condition is met or max attempts is reached
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify retry_until() stops when condition is met
AddUnitTest: verify retry_until() returns error when max attempts reached
AddIntegrationTest: verify retry_until() works with wait_for()
tasks.impl_notes: Combine retry logic with condition checking
